title: feat: nightly metrics snapshot data plane for mission control
body:
## Summary

PR4 — the last of the landing chain (ADR-0018). The /mission-control dashboard flips from live-API reads to a nightly pre-baked snapshot, making the default visitor experience rate-limit-free and instant.

- **`.github/workflows/metrics-snapshot.yml`** (new): nightly (03:17 UTC) + dispatch. Bakes the 7 REST payloads the dashboard consumes (exact per_page/query parity with the client) + `meta.json` timestamp into `apps/landing/public/metrics/`, commits with `[skip ci]`, then dispatches the Pages deploy explicitly. Pushes over SSH with `RELEASE_DEPLOY_KEY` — the ruleset's bypass actor; the default `GITHUB_TOKEN` is not one and its push would be policy-rejected (benchmark-job precedent, caught by internal security review against the live ruleset). zizmor HIGH-clean.
- **PII guard**: `commits.json` passes a `jq` field-allowlist keeping exactly what the client reads (sha/message/date/login) — structured git author/committer emails never reach the public origin (8 distinct emails in the raw payload, 0 after).
- **Config flip**: `dataSource: { mode: 'snapshot', snapshotBase: '/metrics' }` through the `liveOrSnapshot` seam built in PR2 — per-key live fallback on any miss, so the dashboard degrades gracefully until the first nightly run (no seed data committed here).
- **Honest freshness line**: "snapshot from \<relative time\> · data refreshes nightly" rendered from `meta.json`; page lede updated to describe the data plane truthfully.
- **Owner-reported fixes**: docs-tier white frame killed (html/body reset with `--doc-ink` in `doc-shell.css` — reaches exactly the three DocShell routes; marketing pages verified unchanged), and `pages.yml` gains the standard Next build cache (`.next/cache`, lockfile+source keys) silencing the "No build cache found" deploy warning.
- Tests: 108 landing tests green (snapshot stamp fetch, freshness formatting, snapshot-mode routing in the dashboard mock).

## Review notes

Internal gauntlet: tauri-security-reviewer CHANGES-REQUIRED → both findings fixed (deploy-key credential; PII allowlist proven on real data). Live QA: dashboard served entirely from real baked snapshots with zero GitHub API calls, freshness line rendering, white-frame fix asserted computed-style on all three routes.

After merge: dispatch `metrics-snapshot.yml` once to seed, then verify the live site serves snapshot data.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

* **New Features**
  * Mission Control now loads metrics from nightly snapshots, with live API fallback when data is unavailable.
  * Added snapshot freshness information, including the latest refresh time and nightly update status.
  * Added automated nightly and on-demand metric snapshot generation and deployment.
  * Improved landing-site build performance through incremental cache support.

* **Documentation**
  * Updated workflow, architecture, and Mission Control documentation to describe snapshot-based metrics and fallback behavior.

* **Style**
  * Removed default document margins and ensured consistent dark backgrounds on documentation pages.

title: refactor: port architecture-map passthrough to typed-data next route
body:
## Summary

Closes the deferral recorded in ADR-0018: the 4176-line `apps/landing/public/architecture-map.html` passthrough becomes a real Next.js route at `/architecture-map`, following the agent-system port precedent.

- **`src/data/architecture-map.ts`** — typed data (11 clusters, 142 nodes, 128 edges, FIXES/KNOWN_BUGS/FINDINGS/COLORS/CHIPS), extracted from the original by *executing* its data section and serializing (zero transcription drift; content byte-faithful). Two dead edges referencing removed "conversation" nodes were dropped (pre-existing rot the original renderer silently skipped).
- **`components/architecture-map/ArchitectureMap.tsx`** — static SVG prerendered as JSX from the data; the interaction engine (pan/zoom, hover adjacency dimming, pin/unpin, filter chips, per-node keyboard a11y, help-dialog focus trap) stays imperative in one strict-mode-safe effect. Pan/zoom hands off from the prerendered `viewBox` to a pixel-space transform on takeover (restored on cleanup) — keeps the no-JS baseline intact. Static components are memoized so hover only re-renders the sidebar.
- **URL unchanged**: the export still emits flat `architecture-map.html`; parity gate green. Origin invariant holds (zero external requests, verified live).
- **Drift guard repointed**: `scripts/check-landing-drift.mjs` now checks the data file; `CONTRACT_NODE_RE` rewritten for the object shape (verified it captures the 12 real contract citations).
- **Tests**: 13 data-integrity tests (edge/cluster/FIXES/KNOWN_BUGS resolution, tag validity, `<b>` balance) + 2 render-smoke tests (jsdom renders all 142/11/128).
- The original page's console easter egg is restored — byte-faithful content, now syntactically valid (the original had an unescaped apostrophe and never actually ran).
- Docs: ADR-0018 addendum updated to shipped, ARCHITECTURE_STATUS + CONTEXT.md rows, steward meta-docs repointed to the new claim-bearing data file.

## Review notes

Internal gauntlet already run: frontend-reviewer APPROVE (its two advisories — memoization, easter egg — are applied), testing-reviewer CHANGES-REQUIRED → all four findings fixed (import sort, render-smoke, count floors, `<b>` balance). Live QA in a real browser: fit/zoom-to-cursor, pin/unpin, filters, help dialog, zero console errors, zero external requests.

body:
## Summary

Fix-forward for the AI Review gate findings that landed on #756 moments before merge, plus the requested sticky-comment restyle.

- **HIGH — agency list pre-load guard**: `AgencyCompaniesPreferences` `add()`/`remove()` could fire before the `useJobPreferences()` query resolved and replace the saved list with a single entry; now guarded like its sibling panels (92e5302b), with a regression test.
- **LOW — contract hygiene**: `extraAgencyCompanies` Zod schema gains `.max(500)` mirroring the Rust cap (codegen unchanged, verified).
- **LOW — clamp hardening**: `clamp_split_request` de-duplicates `otherKeys` (first-seen order) before the 32-cap so repeats can't waste slots; includes the Stop-gate-mandated tests (duplicate collapse + full-distinct-capacity under repeats).
- **Docs**: ADR-029 consequences note broadened (bare dash-tail strip can cluster same-company team/department-suffixed titles; recovery = user split), and two fast-follows logged (trigram-set precompute; dash-tail heuristic tightening).
- **CI (owner request)**: the `🤖 AI Review` sticky comment is restyled from a flat table into claude-review-style severity sections (bold `file:line` anchors + Fix lines, 🚫 on blocking, collapsed ⚪ Low advisories). Deterministic verdict logic (ADR-0008) untouched; new subprocess tests per the sibling-script precedent.

Declined from the same findings batch (with reasons on #756): dedup-command rate limiting (accepted advisory; server-side clamp bounds the work).

## Tests

Full `cargo test` + `pnpm test` green through the pre-push gate; scoped: 56 dedup tests, 5 agency-panel tests, 35 scripts tests (4 new), fmt/clippy/lint:strict/typecheck/gen:ipc clean.

title: feat(scraping): cluster cross-board duplicate jobs with source chips and split (adr-029)
body:
## Summary

Implements **ADR-029** (`docs/knowledge/decision-records/adr-029-cross-board-job-clustering-recompute-at-ingest.md`): the same posting surfaced by two boards (e.g. LinkedIn + the Adzuna aggregator) now renders as **one cluster row** instead of two, killing the duplicate noise first-time users read as broken.

- **Clustering is recomputed at every ingest** (pure `scraping/cluster/` module) on top of the existing exact `canonical_job_key` dedup — blocking by normalized company + title first-token (folds `GmbH & Co. KG`-style suffixes, `(m/w/d)` gender tags, umlauts), then trigram-Jaccard ≥ 0.90 (or cosine ≥ 0.92 when both cached vectors exist). Membership is never persisted.
- **Only user verdicts are durable**: "not a duplicate" splits live as pair tombstones in a new `dedup.db` (`DedupStore`), registered in the `Resettable` reset registry and the backup bundle (`dedupTombstones`); splits survive every re-scrape.
- **Notifications count clusters**: autopilot "New jobs: N" = clusters whose members are ALL first-seen this run; a known job resurfacing on another board no longer re-notifies. `minMatchScore` is cluster-aware (best member gates; passing clusters keep all members).
- **UI**: one row per cluster with "also on" source chips (open the other board's posting), an "All sources" split section in the detail pane, an `Agency` flag (built-in recruiter list + user-extendable via a new `JobPreferences.extraAgencyCompanies` single-column setter) with a "hide agency posts" filter. Full en+de i18n.
- **New IPC**: `dedup_mark_not_duplicate` with server-side count/byte clamps (renderer Zod bounds are not trusted).

## Review trail

Author/critic pipeline per repo process: rust-backend-architect + tauri-security-reviewer + frontend-reviewer + testing-reviewer all APPROVE after fixes (server-side clamp; non-interactive chips inside the split-view listbox per APG; five test-coverage gaps incl. a shared `posting_cluster_input` production seam for the acceptance fixture). `/review-security` gate: APPROVE. `/review` 3-pass ensemble + verifier fan-out: one HIGH (split failures resolved as `{error}` payloads showed a false success toast) — fixed with the honest union type + error toasts.

## Tests

`cargo test` 2657 passed · `pnpm test` 350 files / 3873 tests · `pnpm lint:strict` / `typecheck` / `gen:ipc --check` / `cargo clippy --all-features` / `cargo fmt --check` all clean. Acceptance fixtures: GermanTechJobs-feed vs Adzuna-JSON same job → one cluster, two source refs; `Senior … (m/w/d) – Berlin`@`Acme GmbH` ≡ `Senior …`@`Acme`; Senior vs Junior never clustered; split survives two autopilot runs; backup round-trip + privacy reset cover the new store.

Deferred fast-follows are recorded in `docs/knowledge/scraping-domain.md` (split-undo, ingest-time embeddings, agency-list growth, LinkedIn card parse seam, split-view selection re-point).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added cross-board job clustering to group likely duplicate postings and display all available sources.
  * Added “Not a duplicate” controls to split incorrectly grouped jobs.
  * Added agency labels, custom agency-company settings, and an option to hide agency postings.
  * Added persistence for deduplication decisions across sessions, backups, restores, and resets.
* **Bug Fixes**
  * Improved handling of unknown job-board sources to prevent display errors.
* **Documentation**
  * Documented clustering, deduplication, agency detection, and related behavior.

Updates `@tailwindcss/vite` from 4.3.2 to 4.3.3
Release notes

Sourced from @​tailwindcss/vite's releases.

v4.3.3

Fixed

  • Support --watch --poll[=ms] in @tailwindcss/cli when filesystem events are unreliable or unavailable (#20297)
  • Canonicalization: match arbitrary hex colors against theme colors case-insensitively (e.g. bg-[#fff] and bg-[#FFF] → bg-white) (#20298)
  • Prevent Preflight from overriding Firefox's native iframe:focus-visible outline styles (#20292)
  • Ensure theme('colors.foo') in JS plugins resolves correctly when both --color-foo and --color-foo-bar exist (#20299)
  • Ensure fractional opacity modifiers work with named shadow sizes like shadow-sm/12.5, text-shadow-sm/12.5, drop-shadow-sm/12.5, and inset-shadow-sm/12.5 (#20302)
  • Parse selectors like [data-foo]div as two selectors instead of one (#20303)
  • Ensure @tailwindcss/postcss rebuilds when a preprocessor like Sass changes the input CSS without changing the input file on disk (#20310)
  • Ensure CSS nesting is handled even when Lightning CSS isn't run, such as in @tailwindcss/browser and Tailwind Play (#20124)
  • Prevent achromatic theme colors from shifting hue when mixed in polar color spaces like oklch (#20314)
  • Ensure --spacing(0) is optimized to 0px instead of 0 so it remains a <length> when used in calc(…) (#20319)
  • Load @parcel/watcher only when needed in @tailwindcss/cli --watch mode, so one-off builds and --watch --poll work when @parcel/watcher can't be loaded (#20325)
  • Use explicit platform fonts instead of system-ui and ui-sans-serif so CJK text respects the page's lang attribute on Windows (#20318)
  • Prevent @tailwindcss/upgrade from rewriting ignored files when run from a subdirectory (#20329)
  • Ensure earlier @source rules pointing to nested files are scanned when later @source rules point to files in parent folders (#20335)
  • Prevent @tailwindcss/vite from triggering full page reloads when scanned files are processed by Vite but haven't been loaded as modules yet (#20336)

title: chore: bump eslint-plugin-simple-import-sort from 13.0.0 to 14.0.0
body:
Bumps [eslint-plugin-simple-import-sort](https://github.com/lydell/eslint-plugin-simple-import-sort) from 13.0.0 to 14.0.0.
Changelog

Sourced from eslint-plugin-simple-import-sort's changelog.

Version 14.0.0 (2026-07-16)

ES2022 allows string literals as module export names ("arbitrary module namespace names"):

export { yukuTs as "yuku-ts" };
import { "a-b" as c } from "a";

This release adds support for such quotes names. Previously, those were sorted oddly, and the autofix could suggest changes that wasn’t valid syntax.

This is only a breaking change if you use string literals as module export names, and only in the form of that you need to autofix your files.

Thanks to Kamronbek_Juraev (@​KAMRONBEK) for fixing this!

CodeQL Action Changelog

[UNRELEASED]

No user facing changes.

4.37.1 - 16 Jul 2026

  • Upcoming breaking change: Add a deprecation warning for customers using CodeQL version 2.20.6 and earlier. These versions of CodeQL were discontinued on 1 July 2026 alongside GitHub Enterprise Server 3.16, and will be unsupported by the next minor release of the CodeQL Action. #3956
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.26.1. #4019

4.37.0 - 08 Jul 2026

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.26.0. #3995
  • In addition to the existing input format, the config-file input for the codeql-action/init step will soon support a new [owner/]repo[@ref][:path] format. All components except the repository name are optional. If omitted, owner defaults to the same owner as the repository the analysis is running for, ref to main, and path to .github/codeql-action.yaml. Support for this format ships in this version of the CodeQL Action, but will only be enabled over the coming weeks. #3973

4.36.3 - 01 Jul 2026

No user facing changes.

4.36.2 - 04 Jun 2026

  • Cache CodeQL CLI version information across Actions steps. #3943
  • Reduce requests while waiting for analysis processing by using exponential backoff when polling SARIF processing status. #3937
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.6. #3948

4.36.1 - 02 Jun 2026

No user facing changes.

4.36.0 - 22 May 2026

  • Breaking change: Bump the minimum required CodeQL bundle version to 2.19.4. #3894
  • Add support for SHA-256 Git object IDs. #3893
  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.5. #3926

4.35.5 - 15 May 2026

  • We have improved how the JavaScript bundles for the CodeQL Action are generated to avoid duplication across bundles and reduce the size of the repository by around 70%. This should have no effect on the runtime behaviour of the CodeQL Action. #3899
  • For performance and accuracy reasons, improved incremental analysis will now only be enabled on a pull request when diff-informed analysis is also enabled for that run. If diff-informed analysis is unavailable (for example, because the PR diff ranges could not be computed), the action will fall back to a full analysis. #3791
  • If multiple inputs are provided for the GitHub-internal analysis-kinds input, only code-scanning will be enabled. The analysis-kinds input is experimental, for GitHub-internal use only, and may change without notice at any time. #3892
  • Added an experimental change which, when running a Code Scanning analysis for a PR with improved incremental analysis enabled, prefers CodeQL CLI versions that have a cached overlay-base database for the configured languages. This speeds up analysis for a repository when there is not yet a cached overlay-base database for the latest CLI version. We expect to roll this change out to everyone in May. #3880

4.35.4 - 07 May 2026

  • Update default CodeQL bundle version to 2.25.4. #3881

title: feat: landing docs tier — docshell, mission control, agent-system route, next 16
body:
## Summary

PR2 of the landing chain (ADR-0018 + addendum included): the **docs tier**.

- **Tokens + DocShell** — shared `--doc-*` palette + one shell (the agent-system dark hand-drawn look).
- **/agent-system** — 946-line static HTML → typed-data React route (`src/data/agent-fleet.ts`; fleet-map links, belt scrub, intake router preserved; drift guards repointed).
- **/how-it-works** — reskinned onto the shell; copy verbatim (parity-pinned); layer color-coding re-tokenized; the `display:contents`-vs-`body > footer` combinator gotcha fixed.
- **/mission-control** — replaces ci-dashboard (**clean rename per owner decision — no redirect; old URL 404s**; references swept repo-wide). Verdict-first whole-repo dashboard: DORA-lite, release timeline, workflow health, PR/issue triage, CHAOSS starter health, benchmark sparklines, community stats — hand-rolled SVG, cached, `liveOrSnapshot` seam ready for PR4.

title: fix(extension): parse linkedin job ad from captured page dom instead of authwalled refetch
body:
## Problem

Importing a job from a LinkedIn **search/collections view** (`?currentJobId=<id>`) via the extension lost the job description. The extension already captures and sends the page HTML, but the desktop's canonical-URL branch discarded it and server-fetched the canonical `/jobs/view/<id>` URL — which LinkedIn authwalls for anonymous clients — so imports degraded to a description-less posting or a `partial` stub.

## Fix

- **`extension_bridge/import_flow.rs`** — `resolve(canonical)` stays primary; when the result is unusable or description-less and captured HTML is present, gap-fill title/description via the new pure `merge_resolve_with_hint` helper from the **hint-scoped** detail-pane subtree only (`job_root_generic_html`). Whole-document parsing is deliberately never run on list shells: their `ItemList` SEO JSON-LD describes the _first listed job_ and would silently import the wrong one (caught by extension-reviewer during audit; regression-tested). No hint → fallback skipped, existing stub/partial path unchanged. Still exactly one network fetch per import.
- **`apps/extension/src/content.ts`** — the `data-ajh-job-root` hint now tries visible job-detail-pane containers (`[class*="job-details" i]` etc.) before `main`, with a computed-style visibility gate (jsdom-safe) so hidden nodes can't steal the hint.
- Docs: seam notes updated in `docs/SCRAPING_ENDPOINTS.md` + new "Extension-import canonical-URL seam" section in `docs/knowledge/scraping-domain.md`.

## Review trail

Implemented by `extension-author`, audited by `extension-reviewer` (critic-contract): initial HIGH (wrong-job import via whole-document JSON-LD) and MEDIUM (no visibility gate) both resolved; SSRF guards, log path-privacy, and dedup keying re-verified unchanged.

## Tests

- `cargo test -p ajh-tauri --lib extension_bridge::import_tests` → 121 passed (5 new merge-helper cases + JSON-LD-poison regression)
- `cargo test -p ajh-tauri --lib scraping::scrape_url` → 87 passed (new LinkedIn search-shell fixture)
- `cargo clippy -p ajh-tauri --all-features --lib -- -D warnings` clean · `cargo fmt --check` clean
- extension: 513/513 tests, typecheck + lint clean (2 new visibility-decoy negative tests)
- Manual: import from a LinkedIn search view with a selected job → description present; direct `/jobs/view/…` page → no regression.

title: feat(extension): match autofill fields labeled in major eu languages
body:
## Problem (user-reported)

The autofill matcher's free-text tier only understood English labels, so forms labeled in other languages (e.g. German Vorname / Nachname / E-Mail-Adresse / Telefonnummer) were not detected. The autocomplete-attribute tier was already language-agnostic and is unchanged.

## Changes (apps/extension/src/lib/field-signal.ts, plus consumers of one new export)

- `matchNamedKey` refactored to an ordered `{ key, pattern }[]` table (first-match-wins preserved; combined full-name phrases before first/last name).
- Keywords widened for DE, FR, ES, IT, NL, PL, PT, SV, DA (+ NO/FI where words coincide), conservatively vetted — mis-fill risk terms skipped by design.
- `textSignal` now strips diacritics (NFD + fold table for atomic letters ø/æ/ł/đ/ß) so accented labels match accent-free keywords.
- Safety denylists localized in lockstep: the generic-name exclusion (school/company/username) AND the `AMBIGUOUS` list (reference/emergency/contact-person/supervisor/recruiter + sensitive PII like Geburtsdatum, PESEL, codice fiscale, personnummer). Collision-prone short terms (`dni`, `bsn`, `urgence`, `firma`) are word-boundary anchored; a shared `isAmbiguousSignal()` now serves both fill and capture.
- `field-signal.ts` remains import-free (classic-injection constraint); both browser bundles verified.

## Tests

18 new cases across languages: positive fills (DE/FR/ES/PL and more), negative guards (Schulname, nombre de usuario, Name des Notfallkontakts, Affirmative-Action name fields still fill, Fødselsnummer skipped). Extension suite: 505 tests green; tsc clean; Chrome + Firefox builds verified.

## Review

extension-reviewer: initial REQUEST-CHANGES (2 HIGH: third-party name fields mis-filling in German; unanchored `firma` breaking English EEO name fields) — all findings fixed and re-verified APPROVE with fresh repros.

title: fix(applications): standalone apply-by-email generation and scrollable jobs scrape form
body:
## Problems (user-reported)

1. **Apply by email was dead until a resume/cover letter had been generated.** The tab's resume text and job description both fell back to the first saved AI generation record, and the fallback meta hardcoded an empty candidate name and English target language — so with no prior generation the Generate button never enabled, and even when it did, metadata was wrong.
2. **Jobs page: selecting all boards and opening advanced settings clipped the form with no scrollbar** — the header block sits under an `overflow-hidden` ancestor with no bounded height, so the bottom of the form (including the Start button) was unreachable.

## Fixes

- **ApplyByEmailTab**: job description now resolves `application.jobDescription` → saved generation → job URL via `useResolveJobUrl` (same pattern as InterviewPrepTab); fallback meta takes `candidateName` from the contact profile (authoritative source) and `targetLanguage` from `detectLanguage(jobDesc)` with unknown → en; the loading gate covers the in-flight URL resolve; the needs-resume empty state gains a CTA to the resumes page (new i18n key, en + de).
- **JobsPage**: the header/scrape-form wrapper is bounded with `max-h-[55vh] overflow-y-auto` (results list stays the primary scroll owner), with a test-id seam.

## Tests

5 new ApplyByEmailTab cases (standalone meta from profile, URL-resolved JD with enabled-only-when-empty, real detectLanguage German → de, true empty state + CTA, in-flight resolve skeleton) + a class-assertion guard on the scroll wrapper. Full desktop suite 2,379 tests green; monorepo pre-push gate clean; tsc/eslint/prettier clean.

## Review

title: fix(export): keep applicant name out of recipient block and adopt profile casing for signature
body:
## Problem (user-reported, screenshot-confirmed)

Two cover-letter PDF rendering bugs:

1. The applicant's own name rendered in the recipient block below the date — the letter parser's header-skip guard compared the model-written name line (`Saeed Kolivand`) case-sensitively against the stored meta name (`SAEED KOLIVAND`), so the line fell through and was pushed into `recipient_lines`.
2. The signature and letterhead rendered ALL-CAPS whenever the stored candidate name is all-caps (the banded layout's smallcaps styling makes it fully apparent, but the source string itself was caps).

## Fix (apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/export/typst_engine/letter.rs, single file)

- Header-skip compare is now case-insensitive and trailing-punctuation-tolerant, on the markdown-stripped line (catches `**Name**` bold headers) — mirrors the existing signature-title case fix.
- New `prefer_profile_casing` helper: when the resolved name is entirely uppercase AND the contact profile's `full_name` matches case-insensitively, adopt the profile's casing. Pure pass-through otherwise — never title-cases (no MCDONALD → Mcdonald damage). `signature_name` consolidated to the letterhead value (provably identical derivation).

## Tests

4 new parser tests (recipient-leak incl. bold-header variant, profile-casing adoption, no-profile and different-person pass-throughs). `cargo test --lib export::typst_engine::` 134 passed; clippy clean.

## Review

Audited by resume-export-expert: APPROVE, no HIGH/CRITICAL. Tracked follow-ups (advisory, out of scope): (a) `to_lowercase()` compare no-ops for Turkish İ/ı and German ß names (graceful — behaves as pre-fix); (b) the independent DOCX letter parser (`export/docx/mod.rs`) still renders `candidate_name` verbatim, so the all-caps letterhead persists in DOCX export.

title: fix(prompts): omit company lines instead of emitting placeholders when company unknown
body:
## Problem

When `meta.companyName` is empty, generated cover letters and application emails print a literal placeholder — e.g. a recipient line reading `Unternehmen/Company` or `[Company Name]`. The string is model-emitted: the cover-letter FORMAT skeleton exposes a `[Company Name]` slot with no omit-if-unknown rule, and both builders seed `at 'this company'/'the company'` fallbacks into the Role context line.

## Fix

- `cover-letter.ts`: FORMAT skeleton (full depth) now instructs the model to omit the company/addressee lines entirely when the company is unknown and never output a placeholder; the user-prompt Role line names the company only when `companyName` is non-blank (trim-aware), else carries a never-invent instruction.
- `application-email.ts`: same `hasCompany` gate on the opening-paragraph skeleton bullet and the CONTEXT Role line.

Known-company output is unchanged (byte-identical for trimmed inputs; whitespace-padded inputs now trim — strict improvement).

## Tests

7 new tests (unknown-company describe in `application-email.test.ts`; placeholder-ban + Role-line assertions in `generate.test.ts`). Full prompts suite: 512 passing, tsc clean.

## Review

Audited by ai-provider-expert: APPROVE, no HIGH/CRITICAL. Advisory follow-ups (not in this diff): the same `at ${companyName || 'this company'}` seed exists in `application-questions.ts`, `interview-practice.ts`, `interview-questions.ts` — candidate for a shared `roleContextLine(meta)` helper in a follow-up PR.

title: feat: terminal velocity m3 — water, splash vat, deep and blackout
body:
## What

**M3 of TERMINAL VELOCITY** per ADR-0016: scenes 2–4, playhead [0.30, 0.58) — the splash, the deep, and the blackout. 26 files, 2,407 insertions.

- **Gerstner water surface** (`cast/world/WaterSurface.tsx`): bounded analytic patch, derivative-based multi-octave micro-normals, fresnel horizon grading, letter-paved surface layer. GLSL displacement verified term-by-term identical with the tested JS math.
- **In-house VAT pipeline** (`engine/vat.ts` + `SplashCrown.tsx`): full decode contract (frame indexing, inter-frame blend, NearestFilter float textures) driving a procedural stand-in crown — the real Houdini FLIP bake drops in later per the ADR errata. Boundary math unit-tested.
- **The deep** (`DeepScene.tsx`): thin dying god-ray shafts (additive fallback implementation; `three-good-godrays` deferred to the post-chain milestone with the peer-widen plan recorded), sparse drifting papers, limp-figure silhouette, near-black grade.
- **Blackout**: fog closes to near-total dark, single amber point growing below.
- **WCAG luminance-velocity clamp** (`engine/luminance-clamp.ts`): the one documented real-time smoothing exception — self-priming on mount (deep-links into dark scenes render dark from frame 1), converges exactly to pure `f(t)` at rest, slew-capped per real second. Unit-tested.
- **Altitude→depth gauge handoff**, extended camera path, scene lifecycle gating, quality-tier ladders. **127 unit tests.**
- Pruned unused skeleton deps: `troika-three-text`, `svg-path-properties`, `framer-motion`.

## Review + verification trail

- `webgl-reviewer`: CHANGES REQUESTED → all fixed → core contract verified (Gerstner identity, VAT boundaries, injection anchors against installed three, zero wall-clock). The HIGH (clamp mount-flash inverting WCAG intent on dark-scene deep-links) was caught pre-merge and fixed with self-priming.
- **Three rounds of live-browser visual iteration** (agent-browser + dev server): **scrub rewind determinism pixel-verified** (t=0.45 from above vs rewound = identical frames); deep + blackout verified excellent; surface beat reads (storm clears to reveal the water); camera-frustum placement proven numerically (worst-case 22.0° vs 27.5° half-FOV).
- Also killed a stale Turbopack dev cache serving pre-strip RIPBOOK chunks (dev-only; production export was clean).

## Known visual backlog (documented, non-blocking — M6 gate pass)

1. Tower window moiré at near/grazing angles — procedural AA improved it twice; the real fix is a mip-mapped texture-based window pattern (shader-engineer, M6). Present since M2, not an M3 regression.
2. Cold-open framing is placeholder until the home-office scene exists (later milestone) — the fog column reads flat at t<0.05.
3. Crown legibility at the splash beat awaits the real Houdini bake (DCC ownership still open per ADR).

Gates: build ✓ typecheck ✓ lint --max-warnings 0 ✓ tests 127/127 ✓ check:parity ✓ ASCII ✓.

title: feat: terminal velocity m2 — tower canyon and paper storm
body:
## What

**M2 of TERMINAL VELOCITY** per ADR-0016: the canyon scene [0.05, 0.30) — the film's first real environment. 20 files, 1,432 insertions.

- **Tower canyon** (`cast/world/TowerCanyon.tsx`): ONE InstancedMesh forming both canyon walls; procedural sodium-vapor window grids with per-window brightness variance and seeded slow flicker (pure `f(t)`, ~top 12% of windows, smoothstepped — never a full-frame flash).
- **Paper storm** (`cast/world/PaperStorm.tsx`): ONE InstancedMesh (4000/2000/900 per quality tier), analytic two-layer flutter with **exact-derivative normals** so sheets catch the canyon light, procedural 2×2 rejection-letter atlas (256², baked once, SSR-guarded, zero downloaded assets), density ramp thickening through the fall.
- **Night grade**: baked cool-key/warm-hemisphere light rig + sodium-ember→navy fog gradient per the ADR's lighting arc (fixed after a live-browser smoke check caught the first pass reading as daylight).
- **Gauge mechanic** (`engine/timecode.ts`): altitude counts down 520 m → 0 through the fall, then hands off to depth below sea level — the gauge now moves through every scene per the ADR's "depth IS progress" rule.
- **Desk props**: procedural mug/chair/sticky-notes falling alongside. Camera vertigo segment, all pure `f(t)`.
- **72 unit tests** (+ gauge regression pinned at the exact live-repro playhead).

## Review trail

- `webgl-reviewer`: **APPROVE, zero blocking** — verified shader injection anchors line-by-line against installed three 0.185.1, zero wall-clock leakage, instancing init ordering attacked and held, disposal sound across mode toggles. Two advisory tidies applied (single flutter evaluation per vertex; symmetric SSR-initial chrome text).
- **Live browser smoke-check** (real Chrome + dev server): full system renders; timecode math exact (t=0.15 → 00:24); caught + fixed: night grade, frozen gauge, and a hydration warning (proven to be the React DevTools extension — suppressed with the proof in a comment).
- Draw calls: **9 peak** in-canyon vs <100 budget. Deferred by design: FPS trace + flicker-rate-vs-scrub-velocity (gate-auditor, M3), tower interior silhouettes (polish).

Gates: build ✓ typecheck ✓ lint --max-warnings 0 ✓ tests 72/72 ✓ check:parity ✓ ASCII ✓.

title: feat: terminal velocity m1 — scroll rig, playhead, semantic layer parity
body:
## What

**M1 of TERMINAL VELOCITY** per ADR-0016 — the deterministic foundation everything else scrubs on. 29 files, 1,803 insertions.

- **Scroll rig** (`src/engine/scroll-rig.ts`): Lenis rAF-driven from GSAP's ticker (single clock), ONE master `scrub:true` ScrollTrigger, damped playhead that converges exactly to pure `f(scroll)` at rest — scrub down then rewind lands identical frames. 3,000 svh scroll track frozen to pixels at mount.
- **Scene resolver** (`scene-resolver.ts`): pure half-open-interval mapping playhead→scene, unit-tested.
- **Semantic layer** (`src/semantic/`): the full story as crawlable server-rendered HTML — 9 scene sections, every legacy link (download/privacy/creature/GitHub/stores/donate/xattr/byline/foot-nav), JSON-LD SoftwareApplication. Verified crawlable with zero JS in the static export.
- **Copy-parity gate** (`scripts/check-parity.mjs` + `check:parity`): bidirectional link + signature-phrase diff against `landing/index.html`; fails on drift.
- **Experience gate**: WebGL2 + >900px + fine pointer + no reduced-motion + detect-gpu tier; every failure path lands on the semantic layer (verified: no canvas in prerender).
- **Chrome skeleton**: letterbox/timecode/depth-gauge DOM overlay, ref-driven, writes gated on value change.
- **Reduced-motion runtime state machine**: gl-live ⇄ slideshow transitions per the skill contract, motion toggle state set from the resolved capability probe.
- **Placeholder GL world**: camera descending a debug column, pure `f(t)` — proves the rig, no art.
- **30 unit tests** (scene resolver, timecode/depth, svh freeze math, motion machine).

## Review trail

Audited pre-PR by `webgl-reviewer` (REQUEST CHANGES → fixed): HIGH `gsap.ticker.lagSmoothing(0)` global-teardown leak (miss-ledger #713 repeat — dropped the call), plus a11y overlay mounting, motion-toggle state, chrome write gating, start/stop symmetry, scroll-track doc note. Deferred with rationale: parity-on-rendered-output, self-hosted detect-gpu benchmarks (pre-flip item), GL-context reclaim (gate-auditor, needs runtime).

title: docs: add the critic contract and seed the miss ledger
body:
## Why

Internal critics kept APPROVING diffs that the GitHub reviewers (claude gate + CodeRabbit) then found real bugs in. Ten verified misses across PRs #713-#715 in one night:

1. 7 reversed-edge `smoothstep(hi, lo, x)` calls — undefined per the GLSL ES spec, driver-divergent — approved as "GLSL clean" (#714)
2. Cover-hinge rotation sign driving the board through the page stack — approved off a single top-down screenshot (#714)
3. A bake-failure escape (throw-via-setState inside the R3F Canvas) that could never reach the DOM ErrorBoundary — approved by reasoning, disproven by a forced-error experiment (#714)
4. Tear/throw phasing — the free rip piece rigid-translated while the seam was still attached (#713)
5. Global `gsap.ticker.lagSmoothing(0)` leaked past teardown (#713)
6. Scroll-init not exception-safe, leaking listeners into the fallback (#713)
7. GLSL reserved word `patch` breaking a shader only at runtime VALIDATE_STATUS — caught by gate-auditor live, not the code critic (#714)
8. Leaked `</content>`/`</invoke>` agent artifact at the end of a README (#715)
9. `NaN`/`Infinity` passing a `<= 0` guard (#715)
10. Arrow geometry assuming rotation about frame-center when the CSS transform-origin was the shifted card center (#715)

Root causes: critics verify the author's handoff narrative instead of attacking it; they reason about runtime behavior instead of running it; no spec-level UB sweep; no self-refutation step.

## What changed

- **NEW `.claude/skills/critic-contract/SKILL.md`** — the shared read-first contract for every read-only critic (mirror of `author-contract`): adversarial stance (the handoff is context, never evidence) · empirical-verification rule ("reasoned-safe" is not a verdict for error-propagation / lifecycle-under-failure / visual-geometry / timing claims; UNVERIFIED routes to `gate-auditor`) · mandatory **self-red-team** section before any APPROVE · per-domain **spec-UB sweep** checklists (GLSL/WebGL, React/R3F, CSS/DOM, JS numerics, lifecycle/globals — structured to grow) · a **miss ledger** seeded with the 10 misses above (project-steward appends every future externally-caught miss) · coverage duty (sibling critic runs before every push, incl. docs-only PRs).
- **14 critic agent files patched** with a uniform 2-3-line block — read critic-contract FIRST; an APPROVE without the self-red-team section is invalid: `webgl-reviewer`, `extension-reviewer`, `frontend-reviewer`, `testing-reviewer`, `rust-backend-architect`, `ai-provider-expert`, `job-match-expert`, `scraping-applier-expert`, `resume-export-expert`, `code-quality-reviewer`, `tauri-security-reviewer`, `pr-reviewer`, `ui-ux-expert`, and an adapted grounding note in `finding-verifier` (ledgered miss classes must not be dismissed by reasoning alone).
- **`gate-auditor`**: one check-discipline line — any 3D rotation/translation/hinge/throw behavior must be verified from at least one non-default camera angle or via geometric assertion, never a single default-view screenshot.
- **`CLAUDE.md`**: one sentence in the agent-system section — critics load `critic-contract` before reviewing.
- Lesson persisted to the (git-ignored) lessons log recording the miss pattern and the fix.

- `01`–`03` unchanged in intent (private / pair / import).
- **`04-fill-forms.png`** — connected popup, arrow → **Fill this form**, showing the real post-fill message `Filled 4 fields — review them on the page.` (msg--ok).
- **`05-answers.png`** — connected popup with **Answer tools** open: a pasted question, the **Help me answer…** button (arrow target), and the full drafted answer + **Copy** button in frame.

## Why

The store listing predated opt-in autofill and the AI answer tools; the gallery now reflects what the extension actually does.

## How

- Two new forced popup states (`fill`, `answers`) captured from the REAL built popup (`popup.js` network-blocked, `popup.css` applied), same DOM-forcing approach as the existing three.
- The `answers` card is ~2× the 800px frame (open disclosure + draft), so a per-shot `cardAnchorY` scrolls it to keep the draft in view — the same `overflow:hidden` crop-to-fit the centred shots already rely on, just anchored on the draft region. The PR-11 rewrite sub-panel is hidden to keep the shot focused on drafting (a curated feature shot, not a claim it's absent).
- All strings are the real user-facing ones (`resolveFillResponse`, `Draft ready — review before using it.`); the drafted answer is generic placeholder text, not real user data.

- `05`: "stuck on 'why do you want to work here?' — it drafts an answer from your resume. copy it when you're happy."

No "AI writes your application" overstatement. Autofill is opt-in, fills only empty visible fields on command, never submits; the draft is from your own résumé and is copy-only.

title: feat: ripbook m2 - kraft paper material system
body:
## What

M2 of the RIPBOOK series (ADR 0015): the paper becomes real.

- **Bake pipeline** (at load, behind the loader): kraft paper albedo+roughness and height-derived normal RTs (4096²/2048² by tier), stain (1024²), smudge (512²), channel-packed hatch (2048²) — domain-rotated fbm (no tiling), ruled lines + editor-red margin, mips + max anisotropy. ONE shared bake; per-page variation via `uPageSeed` transforms. Measured total ~460ms software-render (budget 1500ms). Exception-safe: try/finally + boot fallback to legacy.
- **Page material v2:** consumes the bakes, normal-mapped light catch, seeded stains/smudges at 6-10%; all M1 rip vertex behavior untouched (phased tearP contract).
- **Ink system proven on test geometry:** 3-band toon (bare/hatch/cross-hatch) with object-space tri-planar hatch, distance compensation, blue-noise pencil grain inside strokes, and a screen-constant (~2.2px) inverted-hull outline with curl-noise boil — all stepped off `uBoil`.
- **Notebook + desk:** cover board with hinge-open as page 0's exit (pure f(exitP), scrub-deterministic), page-stack edge, instanced spiral binding (every matrix set), merged procedural desk dressing, quiet desk surface.
- **Pointer parallax** now fed by a window-level pointermove singleton (canvas keeps pointerEvents:none — safe for Lenis and the M6 a11y overlay).

## Fleet review

- **webgl-reviewer: APPROVE — 0 HIGH/CRITICAL** (scrub-safety, lifecycle, allocations, GLSL ES 1.00 correctness, ~199MiB HIGH-tier RT budget math verified acceptable).
- **gate-auditor: full table + delta re-audit all PASS.** Two real bugs caught live and fixed: a GLSL-ES reserved word (`patch`) broke the smudge bake, and pointerEvents:none made the parallax dead code. Fiber tooth resolved at DPR2, zero tiling, smudges/stains visible, 3 toon bands read, boil isolated to ink while camera stays smooth, draw calls flat at 10 (<40), bake ~460ms, fallback matrix intact.

body:
## What

M1 of the RIPBOOK series (ADR 0015): the scroll-rig spine + ONE kraft page that corner-tears and fully reverses. Everything later milestones ride on.

- **Scroll rig:** Lenis owns document scroll (semantic layer stays scroll-height authority) → ONE `scrub:true` ScrollTrigger scrubbing ONE master timeline (duration 9, absolute tweens only) → preallocated `channels[]` (the only GSAP→GL bridge) + zustand store (`getState()` reads in frame code). `?t=` debug jump, `?hud=1` dev HUD.
- **Rip system v1:** row-snap seam pre-split (96×128 plane, seeded fbm corner seam, watertight, attributes `aSeamDist/aSeamArc/aEdgeFlag/aNoise`) + shared `RipPaperMaterial` ShaderMaterial: tear-front gate sweeping `aSeamArc`, cylindrical peel bend, torn-edge boil (stepped `uBoil`), all pure `f(uRipP)` — `ripP=0` reproduces `position` exactly (gapless reassembly). JS owns the free piece's ballistic throw, f(exitP).
- **Experience:** Canvas + raw-composer skeleton (RenderPass only, `autoRenderToScreen=false`, the single priority-1 useFrame owns camera/uniform writes), `compileAsync` warmup behind the loader, GLLoader now really mounts GL behind the capability gate + `flipped()` guard, error boundary → legacy fallback.

title: feat: landing gl takeover p4 - deep fried set-piece and godmode rise
body:
## What

P4 of the landing GL takeover (docs/adr/0014): the one loud set-piece and the recovery.

- **DEEP FRIED**: camera bursts through the descent's black hole into a rotating sunburst world; "WAIT. / IT DOES / EVERYTHING ELSE." slams in per-word (scale-punch); grinning guy + lightning/star/flame draw-ons; secret dialog as set-dressing (P6 wires it)
- **Godmode**: warm gradient-sky rise, paper sun with drawing-on rays, single-sweep stonks arrow, smug guy composed under the crown rule
- **Pass B (nuclear post)**: custom FriedEffect (posterize + 4x4 Bayer dither + saturation push + uv-crunch) with Bloom/ChromaticAberration/Scanline, driven by a monotone trapezoid recipe over [0.36, 0.52] — zero at shoulders, plateau 0.40-0.47. Pass split verified against the 6.39.2 source: CA is the convolution-class effect (Bloom's glow is precomputed), so Bloom+CA share one pass, FriedEffect(mainUv)+Scanline the other
- Staging is lean per the art-direction rulings; the fried post stack is built to full spec (the single loud event)

## Bug found and fixed during the gate

The audit's "godmode renders empty" turned out to be site-wide: postprocessing's autoRenderToScreen pins presentation on the array-LAST pass — which is disabled outside the fried window — so the whole journey outside fried rendered to an offscreen buffer (black). Fix: renderToScreen driven per frame in the recipe, flipping only at the zero-shoulders. Plus an intermediate godmode waypoint so the rise frames its content through the beat, not just the last 2%. Both banked into the webgl-standards skill.

## Gate audit

Ride 0.35→0.65 passes (crash-through reads, fried peaks in [0.40,0.47], clean before 0.36/after 0.52, godmode fully staged at 0.55/0.60/0.62); ramp safety: no NaN/blowout frames, zero self-generated flashes (monotone recipe, code-verified; luminance tracks scroll reversals 1:1, cap holds at human scrub rates); scrub determinism at 0.44 identical; 56 draws / 2.1ms median through the heaviest window; zero context loss over the full ride; fried→godmode handoff verified exact through the ?t= driver (the audit's scrub-desync was harness artifact).

title: feat: landing gl takeover p2 - ink strokes, self-hosted fonts, gl text
body:
## What

P2 of the landing GL takeover (docs/adr/0014): the sketchbook starts drawing itself.

- `scripts/extract-doodles.mjs` + `src/data/doodles.json`: every inline SVG in the legacy page sampled into polyline stroke data (59 SVGs → 54 doodles, 154 strokes, 0 unparsable)
- `src/ink/`: fat-line ink system — `boil.ts` patches LineMaterial's vertex shader with stepped hash-noise line-boil (uniform uploads only, no CPU buffer writes); `InkStrokes` renders doodles per-stroke (hero tier, dashed scrub-safe draw-on via dashOffset) or as one merged LineSegments2 batch (decor tier); fills as earcut meshes
- Fonts self-hosted as TTF (+OFL licenses) with `@font-face` replacing the Google Fonts CDN links — the semantic layer now makes zero third-party requests, honoring the "we don't track you" copy (closes the deferred CodeRabbit finding from #702)
- GL text via drei/troika: per-Text `characters` atlases, `preloadFont` warm-up before Canvas mount, `?fonts=1` debug grid; all 5 handwriting families verified parsing + rendering crisp
- Hero beat staged for real: graph-paper ground, boiling ink guy + dontclick sign, deco scribbles with reversible draw-on, Caveat headline seated on real underline strokes

## The bug this PR also fixes

The gate audit caught the canvas going blank ~1s after boot: `THREE.WebGLRenderer: Context Lost`, never restored. Clean-room diagnosis (instrumented `WEBGL_lose_context` stacks): drei `<Text>` suspends while loading its font → the suspension escaped the Canvas to `next/dynamic`'s Suspense boundary → React hid the subtree → R3F's CanvasImpl treated the hidden-tree cleanup as unmount and scheduled `gl.forceContextLoss()` in a `setTimeout(500)` it never cancels on reveal. The app was killing its own context; it merely looked like a driver bug.

Fix: `<Suspense fallback={null}>` **inside** the Canvas around the scene tree (composer deliberately outside it — reveal-cycle cleanups would dispose it). A `webglcontextlost` watchdog (3s → ExperienceBoundary → legacy page) ships as permanent insurance.

## Verification

- Both routes ~40s / ~19k continuous frames, zero context loss (pre-fix control: deterministic loss at 1.8s)
- Font grid crisp at dpr 2 for all 5 families incl. accented glyphs; emoji/★ scrubbed from GL copy
- Draw-on scrubs forward and back with matching return frames (boil-only diff baseline)
- eslint --max-warnings 0, typecheck, static export build, ASCII scan, zero fonts.googleapis references
- Hero inventory ≈ 34 draw calls; stroke budget: HIGH full per-stroke draw-on (~30-40 strokes/scene), LOW ~12-20 + merged decor path

title: fix: hold the gl experience behind a pre-launch gate
body:
## What

**Recommend merging this ahead of the normal review cycle — it affects live production.**

The Pages pipeline has served the new app's index.html since P0 (#702), which means the capability gate has been mounting the in-progress GL skeleton for capable desktop visitors since P1 (#703) — placeholder beats and all. Per the ruling that production flips only when everything is done:

- `gateVerdict()` now returns `gl:false` (reason `pre-launch`) unless the build is dev or the URL carries `?gl=1`
- Every production visitor gets the legacy page exactly as it behaves today (semantic layer + legacy.ts)
- The GL work stays previewable live at `aijobhunter.app/?gl=1`
- The P7 flip removes this guard (documented in the phase task + code comment)
